pages.text: Every hostile mob in Novus needs darkness to spawn — but darkness is invisible until something crawls out of it. $(italic)Lighty$() makes it visible. Switch on its overlay and every block within reach is marked by whether a monster could appear there.$(br2)It is the fastest way to light a cave properly, mob-proof a base, or find the one gap in a wall of torches.
pages.title: Reading the Light

pages.text: $(bold)Lighty answers to two keys.$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)F8$() — $(italic)Toggle Light Overlay.$() Switches the overlay on and off. This is the one you'll reach for constantly.$(li)$(bold)F7$() — $(italic)Open Light Overlay Menu.$() Opens Lighty's own screen: the mode switcher and the overlay settings.$(br2)Keep the overlay off while you build, then tap F8 whenever you want to check your lighting.
pages.title: The Two Keys

pages.text: $(bold)The colours tell you when a mob can spawn.$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Green$() — safe. There is enough light here that nothing will spawn.$(li)$(bold)Orange$() — dark enough to spawn, but open to the sky. Safe by day; dangerous once night falls.$(li)$(bold)Red$() — dark from block light $(italic)and$() sky alike. Caves and sealed rooms read red, and a mob can spawn there at any hour.$(br2)Light a space until the red and orange are gone, and it stays clear.
pages.title: Green, Orange, Red

pages.text: $(bold)Three ways to draw the overlay$(), chosen from the F7 menu:$(br)$(li)$(bold)Carpet$() — a flat coloured sheet laid over the floor. The Novus default.$(li)$(bold)Number$() — two figures on each block: block light above, sky light below.$(li)$(bold)Cross$() — a plain marker on every unsafe block.$(br2)The same menu sets the overlay's reach, in chunks, and its brightness — handy when shaders wash it out.
pages.title: The Three Modes
